differences.title: 1. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Loads minimal JS upfront, making initial load time incredibly fast.
differences.rightDesc: Efficiently renders components with fine-grained reactivity, small bundle size.

differences.title: 2. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Resumes execution without re-rendering, state persists across server/client.
differences.rightDesc: Updates only affected parts of the DOM with reactive primitives.

differences.title: 3. RE-RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: No component re-rendering on hydration, state is resumed.
differences.rightDesc: Minimal re-renders, only updates necessary DOM nodes.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State is preserved and resumed, no need to re-initialize.
differences.rightDesc: Uses reactive stores for efficient and predictable state changes.

differences.title: 5. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Introduces resumability, a new concept to grasp.
differences.rightDesc: Builds on familiar reactive programming concepts.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on minimizing JavaScript execution for optimal performance.
differences.rightDesc: Provides excellent performance with highly optimized reactivity.

differences.title: 7. USE CASE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for large-scale applications prioritizing initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Great for highly interactive user interfaces.
